vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data confirms a critical failure in the biome. The expected specimens (P1-P4) are absent or reduced to non-viable organic debris.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No String of Nickels visible.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No Mexican Mint visible.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss: Pothos and scale anchor (White Rabbit) are absent.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No Silver Guest visible.

plant_audit.observations: The pots are filled with dark, damp soil substrate, but contain no living plant matter. The presence of white, fragmented material in the foreground pot resembles crushed eggshell or mineral deposits rather than biological growth.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently damp/dark across all observed timeframes.
biome_observations.anomalies: Presence of white, calcified-looking fragments in the foreground pot. No signs of germination or vegetative recovery.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a static, barren environment. There is no evidence of plant growth, decay, or movement over the 5-day period. The biome appears to be a dormant or failed setup.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently devoid of life. The soil appears to be maintained in a moist state, but without any botanical occupants, the health status is classified as 'Systemic Failure'.
vision_report.anomalies: The white fragments in the foreground pot are the only non-soil elements; they do not match any registered plant species.
vision_report.narrative_description: Upon meticulous review of the chronological sequence, it is evident that the botanical registry (P1-P4) does not correspond to the physical reality of the desk. The pots contain only soil and inert white fragments. There is no evidence of the Pothos, String of Nickels, or other listed species. The environment has remained static and barren throughout the observation window.
